meta.description: Visual inspection protocol for validating rendered UI against design system tokens. Used by agents with screenshot/multimodal capabilities.

capture.description: How to capture screenshots for inspection
steps: Navigate to the target page in the browser
Capture component-level screenshots for detailed inspection
Capture in both light and dark mode if applicable
tools: Figma MCP figma_take_screenshot (if designing in Figma)

color_validation.description: Verify colors match design tokens from tokens.json

checks.name: Brand color consistency
checks.what_to_check: Primary action buttons, links, active states, focus rings, selected indicators
common_violations: Different blue shade (e.g., #0070f3 or #2196F3 instead of #346bea)
Hardcoded hex not matching token
Brand color used for non-interactive elements
Missing hover (#0260C9) or pressed (#014599) state colors

checks.name: Danger/error color consistency
checks.what_to_check: Error messages, destructive buttons, invalid field borders, error banners
common_violations: Using warning color (#ffca28) for errors
Different red shade (e.g., #f44336 instead of #dd1f11)
Error banner using bold red (#dd1f11) background instead of light tint (#ffeae9)

checks.name: Success color consistency
checks.what_to_check: Success messages, active status indicators, positive badges
common_violations: Using brand blue for success instead of green
Different green shade (e.g., #4CAF50 instead of #429746)

checks.name: Warning color consistency
checks.what_to_check: Warning banners, caution indicators, approaching-limit states
common_violations: Using orange for warnings instead of yellow (#ffca28)
Warning banner using bold yellow background instead of light tint (#ffebad)

checks.name: Text color hierarchy
checks.what_to_check: Primary text vs secondary text vs disabled text vs hint text. Each level should be visually distinct.
common_violations: All text rendered in the same shade (#212121)
Secondary text too dark (same as primary) or too light (same as disabled)
Placeholder text using primary color instead of #9E9E9E or #bababa

checks.name: Background hierarchy
checks.what_to_check: Page background vs card/panel surface vs hover states vs selected states
common_violations: Panels and page using the same white with no layered depth
Hover state too subtle or too dark
Selected rows not using #E3F2FD
Disabled inputs not using #F5F5F5 background

checks.name: Border consistency
checks.what_to_check: Panel borders, input borders, dividers, focus rings, error borders
common_violations: Input borders darker than panel borders
Missing focus ring (#346bea) on focused inputs
Error border using a different red than #dd1f11
Dividers using a heavier color than #ececec

checks.name: Icon color consistency
checks.what_to_check: Default icons, brand icons, status icons, disabled icons
common_violations: Icons using text color (#212121) instead of icon color (#8891a7)
Navigation icons not using blue-grey (#6c7581)
Disabled icons too dark or too visible

spacing_validation.description: Verify spacing follows the 8px grid from tokens.json

checks.name: 8px grid alignment
checks.what_to_check: All padding and margins should be multiples of the 8px base unit (4, 8, 12, 16, 24, 32, 40, 48, 64)

reference.spacing(4): 32px — large section gaps, form action padding

common_violations: 5px or 10px gaps (not on the 8px grid)
15px padding (should be 12px or 16px)
Inconsistent padding between similar elements
Odd pixel values like 7px or 13px

checks.name: Consistent spacing between similar elements
checks.what_to_check: Same spacing between form fields, between cards, between sections. Measure gaps visually.
common_violations: Different gaps between identical form field rows
First panel has different padding than subsequent panels
Inconsistent margins between repeated card elements

checks.name: Appropriate information density
checks.what_to_check: Not too cramped, not too sparse. Panels should have comfortable padding. Content should breathe.
common_violations: Cramped forms with less than 16px between fields
Overly sparse layouts with 64px gaps between related fields
Panel padding inconsistent across panels on the same page

typography_validation.description: Verify typography matches tokens from tokens.json

checks.name: Heading hierarchy is clear and correct
checks.what_to_check: Page title > section title > subsection > body text. Each level visually distinct and progressively smaller.
common_violations: Page title and section title the same size
Heading levels skipped (H_2 directly to H_5)
Panel titles not using H_3 (18px/500)
Body text larger than section heading

checks.name: Font weight used correctly
checks.what_to_check: Bold only for headings and emphasis labels, not for all body text. Weight 400 for body, 500 for headings/buttons, 700 only for H_5 bold labels.

expected_weights.400: H_1, H_2, BODY_1_BODY, BODY_1_PARAGRAPH, BODY_2, BODY_2_SPECIAL — regular weight for body and large display
expected_weights.500: H_3, H_4, BUTTON_DEFAULT, BUTTON_LARGE, BUTTON_SMALL, BODY_3, BODY_4, BODY_5 — medium weight for section titles and buttons
expected_weights.700: H_5 only — bold weight reserved for inline bold labels and table headers

common_violations: Everything bold (weight 700 on body text)
Headings not visually distinct from body (both weight 400 at similar sizes)
Button text using weight 700 instead of 500
Form field labels bold when they should be regular (400)

checks.name: Minimum text size respected
checks.what_to_check: No user-facing readable content below 12px (BODY_2). BODY_4 (10px) and BODY_5 (9px) are for micro labels only.
common_violations: Error messages or hints rendered below 12px
Table cell text smaller than 12px
Navigation labels below minimum readable size

layout_validation.description: Verify layout patterns and visual structure

checks.name: Element alignment
checks.what_to_check: Labels aligned, inputs aligned, buttons aligned. Vertical and horizontal edges should line up within the same panel or section.
common_violations: Misaligned form labels (some left-aligned, some differently indented)
Buttons not aligned to the content grid
Table columns with inconsistent alignment

checks.name: Content width appropriate
checks.what_to_check: Form fields not stretching full width on wide screens. Tables responsive. Content has reasonable max-width.
common_violations: Single text input stretching 1200px wide on a large monitor
Content too narrow on wide screens, wasting horizontal space
Form panels not using MCFormFieldWidth tokens (HALF, FULL, THIRD)

checks.name: Visual balance and whitespace
checks.what_to_check: Balanced use of whitespace, no cramped areas next to empty areas. Content evenly distributed.
common_violations: Dense form next to empty sidebar region
Uneven column widths in a two-column form layout
Action bar visually disconnected from form content

checks.name: Three-layer architecture reflected in layout
checks.what_to_check: Page shell (MCContentLayout/MCFormLayout) wrapping container logic wrapping pure component UI. No orphaned components outside layout wrappers.
common_violations: Content rendered without MCContentLayout or MCFormLayout wrapper
Missing breadcrumbs or page title from layout shell
Form actions floating outside the layout footer

component_validation.description: Verify component usage matches design system patterns

checks.name: Button visual hierarchy
checks.what_to_check: Only ONE primary (contained, bg #346bea) button per view. Others should be default/outlined or text variant.

expected.primary_action: MCButton2 variant='contained' — single per view, bg #346bea, text #FFFFFF
expected.secondary_action: MCButton2 variant='default' or variant='outlined' — neutral styling

common_violations: Multiple contained (#346bea) buttons competing for attention
Primary action not visually prominent (using default variant for Submit)
Destructive button styled as primary instead of destructive variant

checks.name: All inputs have visible labels
checks.what_to_check: Every form input has a visible fieldLabel above it. Optional fields show '(Optional)' suffix.
common_violations: Placeholder-only inputs with no fieldLabel
Labels missing on some fields but present on others
Required fields not visually distinct from optional fields

checks.name: Status indicators use correct semantic colors
checks.what_to_check: Success=green (#429746), error=red (#dd1f11), warning=yellow (#ffca28), info=blue (#0288D1). Each status visually distinct.
common_violations: Wrong color for status (e.g., red for warning)
No color differentiation between states
Status badges all using the same grey

checks.name: Loading states visible
checks.what_to_check: MCCircularLoader or skeleton shown during data fetch. No blank screens while loading.
common_violations: Blank white screen during initial data load
No loading indicator on button during form submission
Entire page spinner when only one section is loading

checks.name: Empty states handled
checks.what_to_check: Empty tables/lists show a descriptive message, not blank space. Call-to-action (Create button) visible from empty state.
common_violations: Empty table shows blank white space
No guidance text or CTA in empty state
Empty state message not using i18n

scoring.method: Each check: pass (1), partial (0.5), fail (0). Total = sum / count * 100.

dom_extraction.description: For automated validation, extract computed styles from DOM to cross-check against tokens

scripts.all_buttons: document.querySelectorAll('button').forEach(b => { const s = getComputedStyle(b); console.log(b.textContent.trim(), { bg: s.backgroundColor, color: s.color, fontWeight: s.fontWeight }); });
scripts.all_inputs: document.querySelectorAll('input, select, textarea').forEach(i => { const s = getComputedStyle(i); console.log(i.name || i.id, { borderColor: s.borderColor, bg: s.backgroundColor, fontSize: s.fontSize }); });

evaluation_template.description: Template for reporting visual inspection results

checks.observation: string (what was seen in the screenshot)

top_3_issues: string (most impactful visual issue with specific token reference)
